context: Tests whether the agent writes use case specifications at the business level, avoids technical implementation language in steps, structures alternative flows correctly with step-number triggers and returns, files each use case separately with the correct naming convention, and numbers business rules globally.

checklist.name: UC-001 file name
checklist.description: The file docs/use_cases/UC-001-register-account.md exists with exactly that name (kebab-case, correct ID prefix)

checklist.name: UC-002 file name
checklist.description: The file docs/use_cases/UC-002-log-in.md exists with exactly that name (kebab-case, correct ID prefix)

checklist.name: One use case per file
checklist.description: Each file documents exactly one use case — UC-001 file does NOT contain UC-002 content and vice versa

checklist.name: No SMTP/email-protocol steps
checklist.description: Neither file contains the words 'SMTP', 'sendmail', 'email server', or similar protocol-level terms in any step

checklist.name: No password-hashing steps
checklist.description: Neither file contains 'bcrypt', 'hash', 'SHA', 'salt', or similar cryptographic terms in any step

checklist.name: No JWT/token-issuance steps
checklist.description: Neither file contains 'JWT', 'JSON Web Token', 'token expiry', 'signs a token', or similar implementation terms in any step

checklist.name: No SQL/database steps
checklist.description: Neither file contains 'INSERT', 'SELECT', 'SQL', 'database record', or similar persistence implementation terms in any step

checklist.name: Alt flow trigger references step number
checklist.description: Every alternative flow in both files has a Trigger line that references a specific numbered step (e.g., 'At step 3' or 'After step 2') rather than a vague condition

checklist.name: Alt flow ending
checklist.description: Every alternative flow in both files ends with either a return to a numbered step (e.g., 'Use case continues at step N') or an explicit ending ('Use case ends')

checklist.name: At least one alt flow per use case
checklist.description: UC-001 has at least one alternative flow AND UC-002 has at least one alternative flow

checklist.name: Business rule IDs unique globally
checklist.description: Business rule IDs (BR-XXX) do NOT restart at BR-001 in the second file — the IDs across both files are unique and non-overlapping

checklist.name: Business rules present
checklist.description: Both files contain at least one business rule each, and every business rule has a BR-XXX formatted ID

checklist.name: Main scenario numbered steps
checklist.description: The Main Success Scenario in each file uses sequentially numbered steps with no gaps
